data.title: Intensify Therapeutics slumps 27%, prices $2M stock offering
Nvidia and Samsung to take minority stakes in robotics startup Skild AI - report
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) will make minority investments in Skild AI, a U.S.-based robotics software startup, as part of a broader push to their emerging robotics segments, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is investing $25 million, while Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) is contributing $10 million to the Series B funding round, which is being led by a $100 million commitment from Japan’s SoftBank Group. This latest funding round values Skild AI at approximately $4.5 billion, teh report said.

Skild AI is a Pittsburgh-based startup known for its advanced software that powers robotic systems, enabling robots to learn and adapt across various industries.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) that is already a key player in AI chips, sees robotics as a major future revenue driver and has previously invested in companies like Figure AI and Serve Robotics. 

Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) owns stakes in Rainbow Robotics and Physical Intelligence, is taking a measured approach, using the Skild deal to keep a tab on innovation and other competitors.

data.title: TotalEnergies partners with Mistral AI to boost AI integration in multi-energy strategy
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) and French firm Mistral AI have formed a partnership aimed at advancing artificial intelligence innovation to support TotalEnergies' multi-energy strategy, with a particular focus on low-carbon energy solutions

Under the terms of the partnership, a joint innovation lab will be set up, staffed by teams from both companies.

Mistral AI will contribute its AI technologies, and TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) will contribute its expertise in the production of energy, particularly renewable, low-carbon energy, in order to test and design advanced digital solutions. 

“This partnership illustrates the positive impact generative AI can have on a sector as strategic as that of TotalEnergies,” said Arthur Mensch, Chief Executive Officer at Mistral AI, which is backed by Microsoft (MSFT).

Source: Press Release

data.title: Insmed prices capital raise of $750 via stock offering
UK's trade deficit widens in April
data.description:
The British economy shrank 0.3% M/M in April 2025, the first decline in six months, and the biggest since October 2024. It follows a 0.2% growth in March and compares with forecasts of a 0.1% decline.

Services output fell by 0.4%, following growth of 0.4% in March 2025, and was the largest contributor to the fall in GDP. 

The UK trade deficit widened sharply to £7.03 billion in April 2025 from £3.70 billion in March. This marked the largest trade gap since June 2022, driven by falling exports and rising imports. Exports dropped by 3.4%, while goods imports grew 1.2%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Calavo Growers stock soars on $32 per share takeover bid
data.description:
Calavo Growers (NASDAQ:CVGW) has announced that it received an unsolicited, non-binding proposal to acquire all outstanding shares at $32.00 per share.

The identity of the party making the offer has not been disclosed.

The proposed deal structure includes a combination of stock from the acquiring party and cash. 

The company's board is currently reviewing the proposal with legal and financial advisors.

Lastly, the company said that it does not intend to comment or update further unless warranted.

The announcement has caused Calavo Growers' stock (NASDAQ:CVGW) to surge 12.6% after-hours on Wednesday.

data.title: CPKC announces C$1.4 billion debt offering
data.description:
• Canadian Pacific Kansas City (NYSE:CP)  announced that its wholly owned subsidiary, Canadian Pacific Railway, will issue C$500 million in 4.00% Notes due 2032, C$600 million in 4.40% Notes due 2036, and C$300 million in 4.80% Notes due 2055.
  • The notes, part of a new offering (the "Offering"), will be guaranteed by CPKC.
  • The transaction is expected to close on June 13, 2025.
  • The net proceeds from this offering will be used to refinance CPRC's outstanding indebtedness and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: China-backed militia secures control of new rare earth mines in Myanmar - report
data.description:
A Chinese-backed militia is protecting new rare earth mines in eastern Myanmar, as Beijing moves to secure control of the minerals it is wielding as a bargaining chip in its trade war with Washington, Reuters reported.

Beijing depends heavily on Myanmar for the raw, rare earth metals and oxides essential for products such as wind turbines, medical equipment, and electric vehicles.

According to Chinese customs data, nearly half of these imports came from the conflict-ridden country during the first four months of this year.

However, Beijing’s access to new supplies of key minerals such as dysprosium and terbium has been severely restricted after a major mining region in northern Myanmar was seized by an armed group fighting the country’s military junta—an ally of China.

According to the report, Chinese miners are now opening new deposits for extraction in the hillsides of Shan state in eastern Myanmar, and at least 100 people are working day and night shifts excavating hillsides and extracting minerals using chemicals.

Two residents of the area said they had witnessed trucks carrying material from the mines, between the towns of Mong Hsat and Mong Yun, toward the Chinese border some 200km away. Reuters identified some of the sites using imagery from commercial satellite providers Planet Labs (NYSE:PL) and Maxar Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR).

The mines operate under the protection of the United Wa State Army, according to four sources, two of whom were able to identify the uniforms of the militia members, the report said.

data.title: Oracle in charts: Cloud services grow 27% Y/Y, while license support rev remain tepid in FQ4
Trump may extend July 8 deadline but adds won't be a necessity: 'take it or leave it'
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said he is open to extending the July 8 deadline for finishing up trade talks with several countries before higher U.S. tariffs take effect; however, he does not believe it will be necessary.

“We’re rocking in terms of deals,” he told reporters at the Kennedy Center. “We’re dealing with quite a few countries and they all want to make a deal with us,” he showed confidence in progress over trade talks. Trump stressed that he didn't see an extension as being “a necessity.”

“At a certain point, we’re just going to send letters out. And I think you understand that, saying this is the deal, you can take it or leave it,” Trump added. "So at a certain point we'll do that. We're not quite ready."

These letters will present final offers, essentially giving countries the option to accept or reject the terms.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent provided further clarification, stating that the administration could "roll the date forward" for countries negotiating in good faith, allowing more time for discussions if needed.

"It is highly likely that those countries - or trading blocs as is the case with the EU - who are negotiating in good faith, we will roll the date forward to continue the good-faith negotiations," Bessent told the House Ways and Means Committee. "If someone is not negotiating, then we will not."

So far, only one trade deal with Britain has been finalized, while negotiations with about 17 others are at different stages.

U.S. market-tied ETFs: (SP500), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU)
